english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

No one understands me but you.
ugf_translation: find whether each group of words is a group of words that can be true or not true or not. no person get me but you.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

Alligators are on average larger than crocodiles.
ugf_translation: the words: the long animal with sharp teeth that lives in fresh water is most of the time bigger than the long animal with sharp teeth that lives in water that is not fresh.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

Is an alligator a reptile or a mammal?
ugf_translation: Find out if each group of words is a group of words that can be true or not true. Is the long animal with sharp teeth that lives in water a kind of animal with the hard skin that covers the body that is cold to the touch or a kind of animal that has hair and gives the white stuff that a mother animal gives to its babies?

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

An alligator is either a reptile or a mammal.
ugf_translation: Find out if each line is a line that can be true or not true or not. The long animal with sharp teeth that lives in water is either the kind of animal with hard little flat things on skin that is cold to the touch or the kind of animal that has hair and gives white wet stuff that comes from the body of a animal with hair to feed its young.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

Don’t let any reptiles into the house.
ugf_translation: Find out if each group of words is the thing that can be right or wrong or not. Do not let the long animals with hard flat parts on the skin that are cold to the touch into the building.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

You may kill any reptile you see in the house.
ugf_translation: Find out if each line is a line that can be true or not true. You can kill any cold-blooded animal with a long body that lives in the house.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

East Africans are not the best distance runners.
ugf_translation: people from the east part of the big land are not the best far run people

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

Obama is not a Democrat.
ugf_translation: Find if each line is a line that can be true or not true or not. The old leader of the big country with many people is not a member of the group that wants the group that runs the country to help people more.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

Some humans have wings.
ugf_translation: Find out if each group of words is a group of words that can be true or not true or not. Some humans have wings.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

Some things with wings cannot fly.
ugf_translation: Find out if each group of words is a group of words that can be true or not. Some things that have wing can not go in air.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

Was Obama born in Kenya or Hawaii?
ugf_translation: find whether each group of words is a group of words that can be true or not or not. Was the old leader of the country made of many states born in the far away country or the group of land in water that are part of the country made of many states?

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

Oh no! A grizzly bear!
ugf_translation: Find if each group of words is a thing that can be true or not true or not. No! A big dark bear that lives in woods.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

Meet me in St. Louis.
ugf_translation: Find whether each group of words is a group that can be true or not true or not. Meet me in the city on the big river in the middle of the country.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

We met in St. Louis yesterday.
ugf_translation: Find if each group of words is a group of words that can be right or wrong or not. We met in the big city by the big river before today.

english_source:
Identify whether each sentence is a statement (something that can be true or false) or not.

I do not want to meet a grizzly bear in the wild. 4
ugf_translation: Find out if each line is a line that can be true or not true or not. I do not want to meet a big brown animal with sharp teeth that lives in the woods.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

The woman in the hat is not a witch since witches have long noses and she doesn’t have a long nose.
ugf_translation: show whether each piece of writing is a talk that tries to prove something or not. if it is, show its main point. the woman with a hat is not the kind of person that many say can do strange power because the kind of person that many say can do strange power have a long nose and she does not have a long nose.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

I have been wrangling cattle since before you were old enough to tie your own shoes.
ugf_translation:
Find out if each piece of writing is a talk that tries to show a point or not. If it is, find its final point.
I have been working with big animals that give white drink since before you were big enough to tie your own foot coverings.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

Albert is angry with me so he probably won’t be willing to help me wash the dishes.
ugf_translation: the person who is angry with me so he probably will not be willing to help me wash the flat things we put food on

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

First I washed the dishes and then I dried them.
ugf_translation:
Find if each part of words is a talk that tries to prove a point or not. If it is, find the final point.

First I washed the flat things you eat on and the round deep things you put food in and then I dried them.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

If the road wasn’t icy, the car wouldn’t have slid off the turn.
ugf_translation: Find out if each part is a reason talk or not. If it is, find its end point. If the road was not covered in hard cold water, the metal box would not have moved to the side and left the bend.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

Albert isn’t a fireman and he isn’t a fisherman either.
ugf_translation: The person we talk about is not a person who puts out fire and is not a person who catches fish either.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

Are you seeing that rhinoceros over there? It is huge!
ugf_translation: Find if each piece of writing is a reason talk or not. If it is, find its main idea. Do you see the big animal with a sharp part on its nose over there? It is very big!

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

The fact that obesity has become a problem in the U.S. is shown by the fact that obesity rates have risen significantly over the past four decades.
ugf_translation: Find out if each piece of writing is a reason talk or not. If it is, find the main point. The idea that too much body weight is a trouble in the big country of the north is shown by the idea that the number of people with too much body weight has gone up a lot in the last four ten years.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

Bob showed me a graph with the rising obesity rates and I was very surprised to see how much they’ve risen.
ugf_translation: Find out if each part is a reason talk or not. If it is, find its end point. The person showed me a picture with lines with the rising the thing of having too much body weight and I was very surprised to see how much they have risen.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

Albert isn’t a fireman because Albert is a Greyhound, which is a kind of dog, and dogs can’t be firemen.
ugf_translation: Find out if each short piece of writing is a reasoned idea or not. If it is, find its main point. The man is not a person who puts out fire because the man is a fast running dog, which is a kind of dog, and dogs cannot be people who put out fire.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

Charlie and Violet are dogs and since dogs don’t sweat, it is obvious that Charlie and Violet don’t sweat.
ugf_translation: Say if each part is a reason talk or not. If it is, say what the end point is. The first dog and the second dog are dogs and because dogs do not make water from skin, it is clear that the first dog and the second dog do not make water from skin.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

The reason I forgot to lock the door is that I was distracted by the clown riding a unicycle down our street while singing Lynyrd Skynyrd’s “Simple Man.”
ugf_translation:
Find if each part is a talk that tries to prove something or not. If it is, find the thing it tries to prove.

The cause I did not remember to close the door is that I was taken away by the funny man with a red nose who was on a one wheel thing on our road while making sound of words from the song called Simple Man by the music group that sings about a simple person.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

What Bob told you is not the real reason that he missed his plane to Denver.
ugf_translation: Find out if each short piece is a reason talk or not. If it is, find the last idea. The thing that the man we are talking about said to you is not the true cause that he did not catch his flying machine to the big city in the mountains.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

Samsung stole some of Apple’s patents for their smartphones, so Apple stole some of Samsung’s patents back in retaliation.
ugf_translation:
Say if each short piece of writing is a talk that tries to show why or not. If it is, say what the last idea is.

The big maker of phones that also makes the small computers you can hold in a hand stole some of the ideas of the big maker of phones that also makes many other things for their phones, so the big maker of phones that also makes the small computers you can hold in a hand stole some of the ideas of the first big maker of phones back because they wanted to get back.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or not. If it is, identify its conclusion.

No one who has ever gotten frostbite while climbing K2 has survived to tell about it, therefore no one ever will. 8
ugf_translation: Find out if each part is a talk that tries to prove something or not. If it is, find the main idea. No person who has ever got cold hurt on skin while going up the very high mountain that is the second highest on the world has lived to talk about it, so no person ever will.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

Wanda rode the bus today because her car was in the shop.
ugf_translation: a kind of writing that tells why something is true: the person rode the bus today since her car was in the shop.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

Since Wanda doesn’t have enough money in her bank account, she has not yet picked up her car from the shop.
ugf_translation: Since the person does not have enough money in the bank, the person has not yet taken the car from the shop.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

Either Bob or Henry rode the bus to work today. But it wasn’t Henry because I saw him riding his bike to work. Therefore, it was Bob.
ugf_translation: Find out if each part is a talk that tries to prove something or a talk that tells why something is. One of the two men used the big road thing to go to work today. But it was not the second man because I saw the second man using his two wheel thing to go to work. So it was the first man.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

It can’t be snowing right now since it only snows when it is 32 degrees or below and right now it is 40 degrees. 10
ugf_translation: It can not be snow now because snow only is when the air is 32 the hot cold number for air or less and the air now is 40 the hot cold number for air.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

The reason some people with schizophrenia hear voices in their head is that the cognitive mechanism that monitors their own self-talk is malfunctioning and they attribute their own self-talk to some external source.
ugf_translation: Find out if each part is a fight of ideas or a showing of why. The why that some people with the mind problem that makes them hear sounds in their own head is that the part of the brain that watches their own inside talk is broken and they think the inside talk comes from something outside them.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

Fracking should be allowed because, although it does involve some environmental risk, it reduces our dependence on foreign oil and there is much greater harm to the environment due to foreign oil drilling than there is due to fracking.
ugf_translation: the rock breaking to get the black stuff that makes cars go should be allowed because even if it has some chance of bad to the world around us it makes us need less the black stuff that makes cars go from outside and there is more bad thing to the world around us from the black stuff that makes cars go taking out from the ground from outside than from rock breaking to get the black stuff that makes cars go

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

Wanda could not have ridden the bus today because today is a city-wide holiday and the bus service is not operating.
ugf_translation: The woman could not ride the bus today because today is a day when many people do not work and the bus does not run.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

The Tigers lost their star pitcher due to injury over the weekend, therefore the Tigers will not win their game against the Pirates.
ugf_translation: The big cat team lost their best one who throws because he got hurt on the two days at the end of the week, so the big cat team will not win their game against the boat people who take things.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

No one living in Pompeii could have escaped before the lava from Mt. Vesuvius hit. The reason is simple: the lava was flowing too fast and there was nowhere to go to escape it in time.
ugf_translation: no one who lived in the old city that was under the big mountain that blows hot rock that flows like water could have gotten away before the hot rock that flows like water from the big mountain hit. the reason is simple: the hot rock that flows like water was moving too fast and there was no place to go to get away in time.

english_source:
Identify whether each passage is an argument or an explanation.

The reason people’s allergies worsen when they move to Cincinnati is that the pollen count in Cincinnati is higher than almost anywhere else in the surrounding area.
ugf_translation: Find out if each part is a talk that tries to prove something or a talk that tries to tell why. The reason people nose trouble get worse when they go to the big river city is that the tiny dust from green things in that city is more than most close places.
